THE FINAL WEIGHT OF THE FRAME, $ W_f $, CAN BE EXPRESSED AS $ W_f = W \times (1 - 0.02)^n $, WHERE $ W $ IS THE BASE WEIGHT OF THE FRAME AND $ N $ IS THE NUMBER OF ADVANCED COMPONENTS ADDED. EACH COMPONENT REDUCES THE WEIGHT BY 2%, AND THIS REDUCTION IS APPLIED SUCCESSIVELY.

TO ACHIEVE A TOTAL REDUCTION OF ENERGY EXPENDITURE BY 25%, THE FUNCTION $ E(n) = 1 - (1 - 0.02)^n $ MUST EQUAL OR EXCEED 0.25. SOLVING $ 1 - (0.98)^n \geq 0.25 $, WE GET $ (0.98)^n \leq 0.75 $. TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES, $ n \times \ln(0.98) \leq \ln(0.75) $. DIVIDING BOTH SIDES BY $ \ln(0.98) $, WHICH IS NEGATIVE, REVERSES THE INEQUALITY: $ n \geq \ln(0.75) / \ln(0.98) \approx 13.51 $. SINCE $ N $ MUST BE AN INTEGER, THE MINIMUM NUMBER OF COMPONENTS REQUIRED IS 14.

THE CLOCK TICKS FORWARD AS THE RIDE APPROACHES, AND THE CLOCK REMINDS ALEX TO STAY FOCUSED ON THE GOAL. THE CLOCK ALSO MEASURES THE TIME SPENT PREPARING, ENSURING EVERY DETAIL IS PERFECT BEFORE THE EVENT.
